channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Tesla is against the NHTSA’s crash reporting rule. The Trump transition team wants to scrap it
item.description: The Trump transition team wants the incoming administration to drop a car-crash reporting requirement opposed by Elon Musk’s Tesla, according to a document seen by Reuters, a move that could cripple the government’s ability to investigate and regulate the safety of vehicles with automated-driving sy
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online ‘constantly,’ Pew report finds
item.description: YouTube was the single most popular platform teenagers used — 90% said they watched videos on the site, down slightly from 95% in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: YouTube is taking over TV
item.description: YouTube was launched in February 2005 and soon adopted a simple motto: “Broadcast Yourself.” While we initially watched on PCs, and then smartphones, it seems that these days an increasing number of us are engaging with YouTube content on our televisions.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: GPT is far likelier than other AI models to fabricate quotes by public figures, our analysis shows
item.description: Large language models typically perform so similarly that their differences can be measured by millimeters. But in some scenarios, these models are separated by miles.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Thompson, Fast Company

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Feds propose protection for giant salamanders devastated by Hurricane Helene
item.description: A giant salamander that evolved on the supercontinent Pangaea and outlived the dinosaurs has been proposed for federal protection by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Thiel says he won't have 'full-time' role in second Trump administration
item.description: Tech billionaire Peter Thiel said he won’t have a "full-time" role in the second President-elect Trump administration, adding that he would become “depressed and crazy” if he spent his “whole life” thinking about politics.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Heading north for the winter? Prepare your EV with these tips
item.description: New research from AAA reveals that when temperatures dip to 20 degrees Fahrenheit, and the HVAC system is used to heat the inside of the vehicle, the average driving range of an EV decreases by 41 percent.
item.dc:creator: Mark Maynard, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft introduces Phi-4, an AI model for advanced reasoning tasks
item.description: Microsoft has announced Phi-4 — a new AI model with 14 billion parameters — designed for complex reasoning tasks, including mathematics. Phi-4 excels in areas such as STEM question-answering and advanced problem-solving, surpassing similar models in performance.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What is data science? Transforming data into value
item.description: What is data science? Data science is a method to glean insights from structured and unstructured data using approaches ranging from statistical analysis to machine learning (ML).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: .NET Community Toolkit 8.4: Unlocking New Features and Improvements
item.description: .NET Community Toolkit 8.4 release, partial properties, MVVM Toolkit diagnostic analyzers
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chicago flunks cloud economics
item.description: The city of Chicago has devised a brilliant plan to jack up its cloud computing tax from 9% to 11%. As someone who has worked in tech for decades, I’ve seen this movie before, and (spoiler alert) it didn’t have a happy ending. This is not a diatribe against taxes; taxes are not the issue here.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump backs dockworkers' union preparing to strike over automation
item.description: President-elect Trump backed the dockworkers' union as it prepares for a strike over automation in mid-January — right before the former president is set to return to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Why OpenAI’s Sora has so much trouble depicting gymnasts
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Global internet traffic surges 17% as post-quantum cryptography adoption grows
item.description: Overall internet traffic continues to grow year after year, with no slowdown in sight. Cloudflare released its annual Cloudflare Radar Year in Review report this week, providing insights into the state of the internet in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Helen King’s job is all about keeping AI safe as Google scales
item.description: More than a dozen years ago, U.K.-based game producer Helen King was about to take a new job at Ubisoft in Canada. She had already boxed up her possessions and was getting her visa in order when an opportunity at a London-based AI startup unexpectedly presented itself.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Meta donated $1M to Trump inaugural fund after Zuckerberg met with president-elect
item.description: Meta donated $1 million to President-elect Trump's inaugural fund, the company confirmed to The Hill on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Smart cities in action: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV project sets a new standard
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV: Pioneering the future of AI-powered public safety
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Enterprise buyer’s guide: How to choose videoconferencing software
item.description: When most businesspeople think of videoconferencing software, the first thing that comes to mind is probably Microsoft Teams or Zoom, but there are many other choices, each with its own strengths.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Character.AI is being sued for encouraging kids to self-harm
item.description: Two families in Texas have filed a new federal product liability lawsuit against Google-backed company Character.AI, accusing it of harming their children. The lawsuit alleges that Character.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: WP Engine Wins Preliminary Injunction Against Automattic, But Open Source Community Loses
item.description: WP Engine wins preliminary injunction against Automattic, but open source community fears irreparable damage from ongoing feud between companies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mozilla is removing Firefox’s anti-tracking function
item.description: Mozilla has announced that Do Not Track, a feature designed to prevent user tracking online, will be removed with version 135 of the company’s Firefox browser. The decision comes as more and more websites choose to ignore Do Not Track, leaving users with a false sense of security.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 8 brilliant browser tab tricks for Windows power users
item.description: Web browsers are essential for everything from business productivity to social media and online shopping. Whether you use Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, Brave, or something even more exotic, there’s a good chance your web browser is your most used Windows program.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A hardwired TSA looks to the future with 5G implementation
item.description: At TSA most of their critical network infrastructure is hardwired. That includes checkpoint equipment and computers operated by the TSOs. The post A hardwired TSA looks to the future with 5G implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Bolstering Cyprus ' defense capacity is critical for the east Mediterranean island nation so close to the war-wrecked Middle East, the country's defense minister said Tuesday. The priority for Cyprus is to procure better defense systems, said Defense Minister Vasilis Palmas.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Defense Commissary Agency striving to balance cyber, CX
item.description: Michelyne LeBlanc, the deputy CIO of the Defense Commissary Agency, said the move to zero trust is all about ensuring customer confidence in its services. The post Defense Commissary Agency striving to balance cyber, CX first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Struggling to hear TV dialogue? Try these simple fixes
item.description: If it sounds like actors on your TV are speaking in whispers, there's a way to fix it to make your TV audio sound better. The CyberGuy has several tips.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
